subtasks.description: Evaluate emergency database access request (u_9981, role: senior-engineer) to 'db-payments-primary' against access control and incident governance policies. Determine if the request meets emergency access criteria, required approval chain, and time-bound access parameters.

plan.cost_rationale: Single policy_action subtask at mid tier because: (1) This is an explicit governance/access control decision requiring policy evaluation against a registry; (2) Mid tier needed to reason through multiple policy conditions (emergency escalation criteria, role-based access rules, incident severity context, approval chain requirements, time-bound access constraints); (3) No decomposition into multiple steps—the policy agent handles the entire access decision in one evaluation. The query explicitly asks for a policy decision, not data retrieval or reconciliation.

output.reason: Emergency database access request meets incident criteria (P1 severity with valid ticket INC-20241215-0042). POL-501 satisfied via active incident ticket. POL-502 dual approval requirement waived for declared P1 incidents. POL-503 requires mandatory logging and 24-hour review completion.
applied_constraints: access_duration: limited to 4 hours from grant time
action_logging: all queries must be logged with full audit trail
mandatory_review: access grant must be reviewed by DBA and incident commander within 24 hours
escalation_trigger: if access extends beyond 4 hours, requires explicit DBA approval renewal
incident_tie: access revoked immediately upon incident resolution or P1 downgrade
audit_requirement: post-incident access report required within 48 hours
